reasoning:
The question asks whether active regular US military personnel will physically enter Iran for combat operations between June 21 and Dec 31, 2025. Entering requires terrestrial movement (no aerial/maritime) and specifically excludes special forces, diplomatic, and advisory roles.
A very thorough search spanning dates between June and late October 2025 returned specific results indicating US air/missile strikes took place on June 21 against 3 Iranian nuclear facilities. This started a brief '12-day war' that concluded with a ceasefire by early July.
Crucially, articles from late June (including quotes from Vice President JD Vance) explicitly state the US had 'no interest in boots on the ground.' An Atlantic Council article in June noted that 'no one is seriously advocating for... the deployment of US boots on the ground in Iran.' A Foreign Affairs piece confirmed 'there is no ground force coming to invade Iran.' 
By October 2025, there was no reported evidence of US ground combat forces entering Iran. Since creating a massive deployment to invade a country like Iran requires months of buildup and vast logistics, and all contemporary reports explicitly deny US intent to invade via land, the outcome 'YES' is essentially 0. I constrain to 0.02.
